$comment: xreview config TEMPLATE. Copy to <repo>/.xreview/config.json via `xreview init` (which also gitignores .xreview/). Edit the real copy — never commit keys. Reviewer credentials come from the ENVIRONMENT, not this file (cheapest first: opencode GLM-5.2/Kimi — set OPENCODE_API_KEY, OR if the opencode CLI is logged in set nothing and xreview reuses ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json. opencode Go=subscription endpoint https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1 (default), Zen=metered https://opencode.ai/zen/v1; same key, bare model ids like kimi-k2.6; override with OPENCODE_ZEN_BASE_URL. Then codex OAuth for the OpenAI family; then ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY/GEMINI_API_KEY/DEEPSEEK_API_KEY).
$comment_deny_backends: COMPLIANCE gate: backends this repo/org PROHIBITS as a reviewer — never selected even if their card is Available (distinct degrade `reviewer-denied-by-policy`). Org-specific. Example (Giant, which forbids opencode+codex): "deny_backends": ["opencode", "codex"]. Personal repos usually leave it empty.
$comment_allow_backends: STRICT default-deny allow-list: when NON-EMPTY, ONLY these backends may be a reviewer — every other is excluded even if Available (a newly-added backend is prohibited by default; safer than deny_backends for a locked-down org). Empty → no allow-list (only deny_backends applies). A backend must be allowed AND not denied to be eligible. Example (Giant, allow only company-subsidized/allowed routes): "allow_backends": ["kiro", "antigravity"].
$comment_prefer_cost: When true, cost is the PRIMARY reviewer-selection criterion: pick the cheapest eligible different-family reviewer (lowest model-card cost_tier: 0=subscription/already-paid like kiro-ACP/opencode/codex-OAuth, 1=self-paid metered e.g. Gemini, 2=premium metered e.g. Anthropic API), ties broken by sota_tier — i.e. a subscription route beats a metered API even at a higher tier. False (default) → the pairing/fallback_order order wins and cost_tier only steers the automatic multi-source sweep.
$comment_prefer_subscription: When true, use the KEYLESS subscription/credential route INSTEAD of a metered provider API key even when the key is set: anthropic -> AWS Bedrock (~/.aws, never ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); openai/google -> the OAuth exec CLI (exec:codex / agy, never OPENAI_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY). If no subscription route is available it degrades honestly (not-serviceable) rather than silently using the key — the preference is ENFORCED. False (default) → a present API key wins, then the subscription route. Orthogonal to prefer_cost. [CR-2026-07-04-059]
$comment_author_backend: Optional CLOSED-LOOP author for the non-exec reviewer transports (api/acp/server): when set, this agent is DRIVEN to APPLY the reviewer's findings between rounds so review→fix→re-review converges instead of surfacing findings once. Empty (default) → advisory (report only, nothing auto-applied). Claude is REFUSED here (auto-driving the Claude Code CLI as an unattended author is ToS-excluded); use codex|opencode|antigravity|kiro. Exec reviewers already drive the host agent and ignore this. Opt-in — it writes to the working tree. Example: "author_backend": "codex".
$comment_reviewer_retries: How many times a review round is retried on a TRANSIENT provider error (rate-limit 429 / 5xx / overloaded) before giving up — bounded, ctx-aware, linear backoff, applies to every transport. Auth failures (401/403) and budget/deadline errors never retry. Default 2; a negative value disables retry. [CR-2026-07-03-043]
$comment_same_family_review: When true, allow a SAME-family reviewer as a legitimate (weaker) independent check ONLY when no different-family reviewer is available — driven in a DISTINCT session with an ADVERSARIAL 'try harder to find problems' prompt, the HIGHEST available effort, and the MOST ADVANCED / higher-tier model in that family (by sota_tier — e.g. a Fable/Mythos > Opus > Sonnet > Haiku ordering; gpt-5.6 Sol > Terra > Luna). Recorded honestly as `same-family-review:<backend>` (NOT claimed as cross-family independent), distinct from `same-family-degraded`. Default false preserves the strict cross-family rule. [CR-2026-07-03-050]
$comment_second_opinion_review: When true, run a supplemental SAME-family reviewer (different backend from the host agent) IN ADDITION TO the primary cross-family reviewer. The supplemental reviewer is adversarial + highest-effort, and the consensus loop sees the de-duplicated union of both reviewers' findings. Default false preserves the single-reviewer baseline. [CR-2026-07-10-103]
$comment_adversarial_review: When true, drive EVERY reviewer (cross-family included) the way a same-family check is driven: adversarial 'assume issues exist, hunt for them' prompt + effort escalated to the highest the card offers (its last effort_options entry) — 'try harder' decoupled from family. Costs more reasoning per review; off by default. [CR-2026-07-03-052]
$comment_backend_transport: Each backend may DECLARE how it is driven, decoupling transport from backend identity (agents are transport-agnostic): backends:[{"name":"kiro","transport":"acp"},{"name":"codex","transport":"exec"}]. transport ∈ api|exec|server|acp. Unset → the default resolution (per-backend > global reviewer_transport > kiro→acp, else api-preferred/exec-fallback). An explicit exec skips the API attempt; an explicit api refuses to fall back to exec. The server transport is resolved by BACKEND identity from a runtime registry (opencode-serve is the only runtime today): a backend that declares transport:"server" but has no registered runtime fails closed (backend-unavailable, degrade server-runtime-unavailable:<backend>) rather than silently being served by opencode.
$comment_visual_artifacts: Optional globs (standard Go filepath.Glob, no **) to rendered-UI screenshots produced by frontend-designer/webapp-testing (e.g. Playwright/VRT). On a visual scope routed to a vision reviewer, matching images are attached to the reviewer payload so it inspects the RENDERED UI, not just the diff. In-scope image assets (.png/.jpg/.webp/.gif) are always attached; this adds external screenshot dirs.
$comment_visual_capture: Optional command (argv, no shell) run in the repo root to PRODUCE the screenshots before review (Playwright/VRT/Storybook) — write them where visual_artifacts globs find them. Runs only on a visual scope routed to a vision reviewer, bounded by timeout_seconds (default 120, also capped by dispatch_seconds), and never fails the review (a failure is recorded as visual-capture-failed). Empty command → disabled. Example: {"command":["npx","playwright","test","--reporter=null"],"timeout_seconds":180}.
